commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh In that you provoke me unto wrath with the works of your hands, burning incense unto other gods in the land of Egypt, where you have gone to dwell, that you might cut yourselves off, and that you might be a curse and a reproach among all the nations of the earth. Their blindness and malice was inconceivable. They attribute their past miseries to the neglect of worshipping the moon. They may be compared to a mixture of iron and brass, which is put through the crucible in vain, chap. , and Ezechiel xxii.
